---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "C:/Users/wjarret/University of Michigan Dropbox/MED-RNA-Lab/Team Members/Wjarret/Lab Notebook/General Data/RNP-MaP Results/Dada2_denoising_URNA_03082026/RNP_MaP_CSVs")
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(scales)
library(ggpubr)
```

```{r}

# 01/29/2026
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

U4_1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U4_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_U4-1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U4_1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U4_1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

U4_2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U4_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_U4-2_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U4_2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U4_2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

U4_atac_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U4atac_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U4_atac_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U4_atac_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

U5A_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U5_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_U5A_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U5A_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U5A_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

U5B_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 <- read.csv('HeLa-U5_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129_U5B_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U5B_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence <- toupper(U5B_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129$Sequence)

# 07/07/2025
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 <- read.csv('HeLa-U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707_ASV2_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence <- toupper(U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence)

U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 <- read.csv('HeLa-U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence <- toupper(U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence)

U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 <- read.csv('HeLa-U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence <- toupper(U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence)

U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 <- read.csv('HeLa-U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence <- toupper(U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707$Sequence)

# 05/25/2025
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250525 <- read.csv('HeLa-U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250525_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250525$Sequence <- toupper(U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250525$Sequence)

# 11/09/2024
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 <- read.csv('HeLa-U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence <- toupper(U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence)

U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 <- read.csv('HeLa-U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_110924_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence <- toupper(U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence)

U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 <- read.csv('HeLa-U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_110924_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence <- toupper(U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence)

U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 <- read.csv('HeLa-U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_110924_ASV1_profile_RESULTS.csv', header = TRUE)
U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence <- toupper(U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024$Sequence)

```

```{r}
#Counting up all the RNP-Map sites, how many are each nucleotide? For all nucleotides that doesn't have a nan and make a pie chart with each percent of measurable mnucleotides vs RNP-MaP sites, how different would they look?

# I am going to use this to create a subclass to show all nucleotides that don't have a nan readout


# 01/29/2026
U1_DMSO_20260129  <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129  %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U11_DMSO_20260129 <- U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U12_DMSO_20260129 <- U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U4_1_DMSO_20260129    <- U4_1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129    %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U4_2_DMSO_20260129    <- U4_2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129    %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U4_atac_DMSO_20260129 <- U4_atac_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U5A_DMSO_20260129 <- U5A_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U5B_DMSO_20260129 <- U5B_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)

U1_DMSO_NaN_20260129  <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129  %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U11_DMSO_NaN_20260129 <- U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U12_DMSO_NaN_20260129 <- U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U4_1_DMSO_NaN_20260129    <- U4_1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129    %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U4_2_DMSO_NaN_20260129    <- U4_2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129    %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U4_atac_DMSO_NaN_20260129 <- U4_atac_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U5A_DMSO_NaN_20260129 <- U5A_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U5B_DMSO_NaN_20260129 <- U5B_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20260129 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')

# 07/07/2025
U1_DMSO_20250707  <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707  %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U2_DMSO_20250707  <- U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707  %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U11_DMSO_20250707 <- U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U12_DMSO_20250707 <- U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)

U1_DMSO_NaN_20250707  <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707  %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U2_DMSO_NaN_20250707  <- U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707  %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U11_DMSO_NaN_20250707 <- U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U12_DMSO_NaN_20250707 <- U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250707 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')

# 05/25/2025
U1_DMSO_20250525     <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250525 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U1_DMSO_NaN_20250525 <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_20250525 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')

# 11/09/2024
U1_DMSO_11092024  <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024  %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U2_DMSO_11092024  <- U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024  %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U11_DMSO_11092024 <- U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)
U12_DMSO_11092024 <- U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 %>% filter(RNPsite == 1)

U1_DMSO_NaN_11092024  <- U1_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024  %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U2_DMSO_NaN_11092024  <- U2_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024  %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U11_DMSO_NaN_11092024 <- U11_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')
U12_DMSO_NaN_11092024 <- U12_DMSO_aq_SDA_int_11092024 %>% filter(XL_rate != 'NaN')

```

```{r}

# U1 <- tibble(
#   Sequence = U1_DMSO$Sequence,
#   Frequncey = (nrow(U1_DMSO) / nrow(U1_DMSO_NaN)*100)
#   )
# 
# U2 <- tibble(
#   Sequence = U2_DMSO$Sequence,
#   Frequncey = (nrow(U2_DMSO) / nrow(U2_DMSO_NaN)*100)
#   )
# 
# U11 <- tibble(
#   Sequence = U11_DMSO$Sequence,
#   Frequency = (nrow(U11_DMSO) / nrow(U11_DMSO_NaN)*100)
#   )
# U12 <- tibble(
#   Sequence = U12_DMSO$Sequence,
#   Frequency = (nrow(U12_DMSO) / nrow(U12_DMSO_NaN)*100)
#   ) 

#Chess <- list( U1 = U1, U2 = U2, U11 = U11, U12 = U12)

# for(name in names(Chess)) {
#   df <- Chess[[name]]  
#   p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Sequence, y = Frequncey)) +
#     geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "skyblue") +
#     geom_text(aes(label = round(Frequncey, 2)), vjust = -0.5) +
#     scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 50), oob = squish) +
#     labs(title = name, x = "Nucleotide", y = "Relative Frequency") +
#     theme_minimal()
#   print(p)  
# }



```

```{r}
count_nucleotides_percent <- function(x) {
  df <- as.data.frame(table(x))
  colnames(df) <- c("Nucleotide", "Count")
  df$Percent <- round(df$Count / sum(df$Count) * 100, 2)
  return(df)
}

measurable_counts <- list(
  # 01/29/2026
  U1_20260129     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  U11_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U11_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  U12_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U12_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  # U4_1_20260129   = count_nucleotides_percent(U4_1_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  # U4_2_20260129   = count_nucleotides_percent(U4_2_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  # U4atac_20260129 = count_nucleotides_percent(U4_atac_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  # U5A_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U5A_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  # U5B_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U5B_DMSO_NaN_20260129$Sequence),
  # 07/07/2025
  U1_20250707     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_NaN_20250707$Sequence),
  U2_20250707     = count_nucleotides_percent(U2_DMSO_NaN_20250707$Sequence),
  U11_20250707    = count_nucleotides_percent(U11_DMSO_NaN_20250707$Sequence),
  U12_20250707    = count_nucleotides_percent(U12_DMSO_NaN_20250707$Sequence),
  # 05/25/2025
  U1_20250525     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_NaN_20250525$Sequence),
  # 11/09/2024
  U1_11092024     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_NaN_11092024$Sequence),
  U2_11092024     = count_nucleotides_percent(U2_DMSO_NaN_11092024$Sequence),
  U11_11092024    = count_nucleotides_percent(U11_DMSO_NaN_11092024$Sequence),
  U12_11092024    = count_nucleotides_percent(U12_DMSO_NaN_11092024$Sequence)
)

rnp_counts <- list(
  # 01/29/2026
  U1_20260129     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  U11_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U11_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  U12_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U12_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  # U4_1_20260129   = count_nucleotides_percent(U4_1_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  # U4_2_20260129   = count_nucleotides_percent(U4_2_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  # U4atac_20260129 = count_nucleotides_percent(U4_atac_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  # U5A_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U5A_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  # U5B_20260129    = count_nucleotides_percent(U5B_DMSO_20260129$Sequence),
  # 07/07/2025
  U1_20250707     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_20250707$Sequence),
  U2_20250707     = count_nucleotides_percent(U2_DMSO_20250707$Sequence),
  U11_20250707    = count_nucleotides_percent(U11_DMSO_20250707$Sequence),
  U12_20250707    = count_nucleotides_percent(U12_DMSO_20250707$Sequence),
  # 05/25/2025
  U1_20250525     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_20250525$Sequence),
  # 11/09/2024
  U1_11092024     = count_nucleotides_percent(U1_DMSO_11092024$Sequence),
  U2_11092024     = count_nucleotides_percent(U2_DMSO_11092024$Sequence),
  U11_11092024    = count_nucleotides_percent(U11_DMSO_11092024$Sequence),
  U12_11092024    = count_nucleotides_percent(U12_DMSO_11092024$Sequence)
)

comparison_list <- list()

for (name in names(rnp_counts)) {
  rnp <- rnp_counts[[name]]
  meas <- measurable_counts[[name]]
  
  merged <- full_join(rnp, meas, by = "Nucleotide", suffix = c("_RNP", "_Meas")) %>% mutate(
      Enrichment = round(Percent_RNP  / Percent_Meas, 2),
      Dataset = name
    ) 
  
  comparison_list[[name]] <- merged
}

comparison_df <- bind_rows(comparison_list)

# Parse snRNA identity from the dataset name (e.g. "U1_20260129" -> "U1")
comparison_df$snRNA <- sub("_.*", "", comparison_df$Dataset)

# Keep Dataset as the unique replicate identifier
comparison_df$Dataset <- factor(comparison_df$Dataset)

# snRNA as ordered factor for plotting
comparison_df$snRNA <- factor(comparison_df$snRNA, 
                               levels = c("U1", "U2", "U4", "U4atac", "U5A", "U5B", "U11", "U12"))


# 
# x_row = names(comparison_df)
# y_val = colMeans(comparison_df$Enrichment, na.rm = TRUE)
# sd_val = sapply(comparison_df$Enrichment, sd, na.rm = TRUE)
# df = data.frame(x_name = x_row, mean = y_val, sd = sd_val)
# 
# ggplot(df, aes(x= x_row, y = y_val )) + 
#   geom_bar(stat = 'identity')+
#   geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean-sd, ymax=mean+sd), width=.2)+
#   labs(title = "URNA Nucleotide Frequency Ratio",
#        y = "Ratio (RNP % / Measurable %)",
#        x = "Nucleotide")


```

```{r}
# Per Nucleotide only (for global mean/SD overlay)
nucleotide_summary <- comparison_df %>%
  group_by(Nucleotide) %>%
   summarise(
    mean_enrich = mean(Enrichment, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_enrich   = sd(Enrichment, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

Do perform the pairwise comparison data, I chose a Kruskal-Wallis test with a Dunn's multiple comparisons test. The data for each nucleotide was shown to be non-normal ( no sample passed the shapiro-wilks test) leading to the performance of a non-parametric test. The compare samples with difference in variation and are non-normal, the Kruskal-wallis test fits best. The test works by ranking all values from low to high, not paying attention to groups each value belongs to, and then takes the rank sum per group. Discrepancies between each rank sum is take to provide the (H)-statistic. The larger the (H)-statistic, the larger the discrepancies. A P-value is then calculated to determine if their is a difference due to random sampling, with a small p-value concluding that populations have different distributions. If the p-value proves to be small, a post-hoc Dunn's test is performed. This test compares the differences in the sum of ranks between 2 columns with the expected average difference.

![](images/clipboard-2311344833.png){width="464"}

```{r}
#Shaprio-wilks
comparison_df %>%
  group_by(Nucleotide) %>%
  summarise(
    p_value = shapiro.test(Enrichment)$p.value,
    n = n()
  )

#Data was not normal so lets use kruskal-wallis
kruskal.test(
  x = comparison_df$Enrichment,
  g = comparison_df$Nucleotide,
  )

#post-hoc test
dunn.test::dunn.test(
  x = comparison_df$Enrichment,
  g = comparison_df$Nucleotide,
  method = "bonferroni")
```

```{r}

dunns_test_tiddy <- comparison_df %>%
 dunn_test(Enrichment ~ Nucleotide, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni") %>%
 add_significance() %>%
 filter(p.adj < 0.05)

ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = Nucleotide, y = Enrichment)) +
  geom_point(
    aes(color = snRNA),
    size = 3, alpha = 0.7,
    position = position_jitter(width = 0.1, seed = 42)
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data        = nucleotide_summary,
    aes(x = Nucleotide, y = mean_enrich),
    size        = 5,
    shape       = 95,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  # geom_errorbar(
  #   data        = nucleotide_summary,
  #   aes(x = Nucleotide, ymin = mean_enrich - sd_enrich, ymax = mean_enrich + sd_enrich),
  #   color       = "black",
  #   width       = 0.2,
  #   linewidth   = 0.8,
  #   inherit.aes = FALSE
  # ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    dunns_test_tiddy,
    label         = "p.adj.signif",
    y.position    = max(comparison_df$Enrichment, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.1,
    step.increase = 0.1,
    inherit.aes   = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title   = "snRNA Nucleotide Frequency Ratio",
    y       = "Ratio (RNP % / Measurable %)",
    x       = "Nucleotide",
    color   = "snRNA"
  ) +
  theme_classic()


```
